prompt: I need to write a memo to the exec team explaining why we missed our Q4 number and what we're doing about it. We missed by 18%. The honest answer is partly external (market softened in November) and partly internal (we made a pricing change that hurt close rates). The vibe needs to be honest but not panicked, and I can't throw the sales team under the bus.

assertions.text: Leads with the news (the 18% miss) in the first line or paragraph, not buildup
Explicitly chooses a failure frame and explains why (likely 'sign of the times' + 'system breakdown' over 'someone betrayed us')
Names both causes (external softening + internal pricing change) without scapegoating
Ends with concrete asks or next steps, not just analysis
Tone is honest-not-panicked as requested (no apology spiral, no dismissive minimization)
Produces a memo structure (sections or clear paragraphs) the user can edit, not free-flowing prose
